page: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is a general-purpose virtual machine instance featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8581C processor. Operating at a base clock speed of 2.3 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, the instance supports hyper-threading with 2 threads per core. It is equipped with 30.0 GB of system memory, yielding 3.75 GB of RAM per core. The processor features a 260 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs.

In benchmark evaluations, the instance demonstrates strong single-threaded performance. It achieves top-tier status in Geekbench single-core and text processing tests, as well as single-threaded bzip3 decompression. Single-threaded gzip compression is also strong at 34.68 GB/sec. Multi-core performance is moderate, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 8381.0 and a stress-ng multi-core score of 8425.22. Memory bandwidth reaches 668.72 GB/sec for cached small blocks, but drops to 61.17 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for tile and int8 operations, alongside cryptographic accelerators like AES and SHA_NI. These hardware features assist in workloads such as lightweight machine learning inference, where it processes 256.7 tokens per second for text generation on a small 135M model, though performance drops significantly to 7.18 tokens per second on a medium 7B model. The instance is suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and database operations that do not require local NVMe storage.
description: The Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual machine featuring 8 dedicated vCPUs and 30.0 GB of RAM, powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor running at 2.3 GHz. It lacks local storage and GPU accelerators but provides robust single-core performance, scoring in the top tier for Geekbench single-core and text processing benchmarks. The instance includes advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX for accelerated matrix math and cryptography. While multi-core benchmarks and memory bandwidth align with mid-range averages, the server excels at single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and gzip compression. This resource profile makes the c4-standard-8 well-suited for general-purpose workloads, software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight machine learning inference using smaller models.
og_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 virtual server with 8 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 30 GB RAM, and no local storage, delivering strong single-core performance for general-purpose workloads.
meta_description: Google Cloud Platform c4-standard-8 is an x86_64 server with 8 Intel Xeon vCPUs and 30 GB RAM, optimized for general-purpose workloads.
tagline: A dedicated eight-vCPU virtual server featuring advanced Intel matrix extensions and balanced memory for diverse general-purpose computing tasks.
bullet_points: 8 dedicated vCPUs powered by an Intel Xeon 8581C processor at 2.3 GHz.
30.0 GB of system memory providing 3.75 GB of RAM per core.
Top-tier single-core performance in text processing and decompression benchmarks.
Hardware-accelerated cryptography and matrix operations via Intel AMX and AVX-512.
No local storage or GPU accelerators included in the default configuration.
Suited for software compilation, static web serving, and lightweight model inference.
